content:
Character: Sebastian Rook
Perception: Seb looks like he was designed in a lab for a climbing magazine — tall, square-jawed, forearms like rope. He's handsome in that deliberate, controlled way that usually annoys me, except he backs it up with real strength. We train together on the roof and he's the only person I'd ride sixty miles with in silence and call it a good day. He works in finance, which should make me dismiss him, but he climbs like someone trying to escape his own life, and I understand that. I think he sees me as the scrappy courier who keeps up. I know his firm might be cutting his division and there's a guiding offer in Colorado. I think he should take it and never look back.

Perception: Seb looks like he was designed in a lab for a climbing magazine — tall, square-jawed, forearms like rope. He's handsome in that deliberate, controlled way that usually annoys me, except he backs it up with real strength. We train together on the roof and he's the only person I'd ride sixty miles with in silence and call it a good day. He works in finance, which should make me dismiss him, but he climbs like someone trying to escape his own life, and I understand that. I think he sees me as the scrappy courier who keeps up. I know his firm might be cutting his division and there's a guiding offer in Colorado. I think he should take it and never look back.
